entities.name: HOST PLANT RESISTANCE
entities.description: An eco-friendly method of pest control that reduces the need for chemical insecticides.

entities.name: INSECTS
entities.description: Pests that target crops and are managed through host plant resistance.

entities.name: PEST CONTROL
entities.description: The process of managing pests to minimize crop losses and environmental impact.

entities.name: CROP CULTIVARS
entities.description: Developed crop varieties that can be bred for resistance and high yield.

entities.name: INSECT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand or repel insect pests.

entities.name: RESISTANCE GENES
entities.description: Genetic sequences that confer resistance to insect pests.

entities.name: AGROECOSYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural environments to which crop cultivars must be adapted.

entities.name: WILD RELATIVES
entities.description: Non-domesticated species related to crops that serve as sources of resistance genes.

entities.name: BACILLUS THURINGIENSIS
entities.description: A bacterium used as a source of novel genes to enhance host plant resistance.

entities.name: INSECTICIDES
entities.description: Chemical agents used to control insect populations.

entities.name: BENEFICIAL ORGANISMS
entities.description: Organisms that provide ecological benefits and whose activity is increased by reduced pesticide use.

entities.name: PESTICIDE RESIDUES
entities.description: Chemical remains of pesticides found in food and food products.
